page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5ne.16xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at a base frequency of 2.5 GHz. The instance provides 64 dedicated physical cores with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, ensuring that each vCPU maps to a dedicated physical execution unit without hyperthreading overhead.

This instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met through network-attached block storage options. Additionally, there are no integrated GPU accelerators, making this instance type strictly suited for CPU-bound and memory-balanced workloads.

For connectivity, the ecs.g5ne.16xlarge offers a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps. This high network throughput, combined with dedicated CPU resources, makes the instance suitable for enterprise applications, web serving, middle-tier application servers, and general-purpose database hosting where local high-speed SSDs are not a strict requirement. The dedicated core allocation provides predictable performance compared to shared-core alternatives.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5ne.16xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor. Operating on the KVM hypervisor with an x86_64 architecture, it delivers 64 dedicated physical cores with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, eliminating hyperthreading overhead. The instance is configured with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It features a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps to support high-throughput network operations. Because it contains no local storage or GPU accelerators, it relies entirely on network-attached storage. This hardware profile makes the ecs.g5ne.16xlarge highly suitable for enterprise applications, web serving, and database hosting requiring predictable, dedicated compute resources without the need for local NVMe drives or hardware acceleration.
